generated_requirements.text: The system shall automatically retrieve and refresh all Event and Activity data from Sync Matrix 1.0 and Exercise Management Tool 1.0 on a fixed 60-second cycle.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-01 mandates a 60-second automated refresh cycle to ensure real-time situational awareness during exercise execution.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a manual pause control that suspends the automatic 60-second data refresh cycle, and a resume control that restarts the cycle immediately upon user activation.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-01 permits manual pause of the refresh cycle to support briefing moments or display freeze requirements.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall implement a Multi-Document Interface (MDI) layout that allocates the left two-thirds of the display area to a graphical timeline view and the right one-third to a data table region. This ratio shall be fixed and not user-configurable.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-02 defines fixed display layout proportions to ensure consistent projection-ready presentation.

generated_requirements.text: Within the data table region, the system shall display sequential (order-dependent, non-time-bound) Events in the top one-quarter of the region, and temporally relevant Events ordered by proximity to current exercise time in the bottom three-quarters.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-03 defines the segmentation of the data table region to distinguish sequential from temporal Events.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall color-code all displayed Events and Activities based on their variance from current exercise time, using thresholds and colors that conform to the DHS color schema standard.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-04 and BR-05 require DHS-compliant color coding to enable instant visual triage of schedule adherence.

generated_requirements.text: All visual elements of the application — including backgrounds, text, highlights, and status indicators — shall conform to the color schema established by the Department of Homeland Security. No custom or ad hoc color choices are permitted without formal DHS approval.
generated_requirements.rationale: RC-01 and BR-05 mandate strict adherence to DHS color schema standards across all UI elements.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display a direct navigation link for each Event and Activity that opens the corresponding record in its originating source application (Sync Matrix 1.0 or EMT 1.0), enabling users to perform modifications without leaving the display context.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-06 requires deep-link navigation back to source applications since EEMDS is a display-only tool.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display only MSEL Summary data in both the graphical timeline nodes and the data table rows. Full MSEL detail records shall not be rendered within EEMDS.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-07 restricts the data scope to MSEL Summary data to keep the display concise and projection-readable.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall authenticate every user at login and deny access to any user who is not pre-authorized by the system administrator. Guest and anonymous access modes shall not be supported.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-08 mandates positive authentication and denial of unauthorized access to protect exercise data.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall simultaneously display two independent, visually distinct clocks at all times: one showing current local (wall-clock) time and one showing current exercise time.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-09 requires dual-clock display to eliminate confusion when exercise time diverges from local time.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow authorized users to define, configure, and modify time segments used to organize Events on the timeline. Time segments shall be configurable per exercise and must be set prior to or during exercise execution.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-10 requires user-configurable time segments to support varied exercise structures.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support filtering of displayed Events and Activities by Venue. The filter shall be applicable independently or in combination with other active filters.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-11 mandates venue-based filtering to reduce cognitive load during fast-moving exercise scenarios.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support filtering of displayed Events and Activities by Key Events designation. The filter shall be applicable independently or in combination with other active filters.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-11 mandates key-event-based filtering to allow controllers to isolate critical events quickly.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall capture a history log of all changes to Event and Activity data reflected in EEMDS, originating from source systems. This history report shall be retrievable at any point during or after the exercise.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-12 requires a complete audit trail to support post-exercise after-action review and reconstruction.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide an on-demand, unfiltered view of all Events in the exercise, accessible regardless of any currently active filters.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-13 requires a full exercise view to ensure controllers can always access the complete event picture.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall integrate with Sync Matrix 1.0 using its existing data interface (API, database connection, or file export) in read-only mode, without requiring any modification to Sync Matrix 1.0.
generated_requirements.rationale: TC-02 and D-01 require read-only integration with Sync Matrix 1.0 using its existing interface.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall integrate with Exercise Management Tool 1.0 using its existing data interface (API, database connection, or file export) in read-only mode, without requiring any modification to EMT 1.0.
generated_requirements.rationale: TC-02 and D-02 require read-only integration with EMT 1.0 using its existing interface.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall render a graphical timeline in the left two-thirds of the display, plotting Events and Activities as nodes positioned according to their scheduled exercise time and current variance status.
generated_requirements.rationale: The graphical timeline is the primary situational awareness mechanism described in the future state and scope sections.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall generate actual versus assumed time variance charts for Events and Activities, providing quantitative schedule variance analysis accessible to users during and after exercise execution.
generated_requirements.rationale: The scope section explicitly includes actual versus assumed time variance charts as a required feature.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be deployable as a Windows-based client application on a standard PC within the exercise operations center, with no dependency on server-side rendering or cloud infrastructure for core display functionality.
generated_requirements.rationale: TC-01 constrains deployment to a standard client PC without cloud or server-side rendering dependencies.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall achieve 99% availability during normal business hours (0800–1800 local time, Monday through Friday) over the first six months of operation. Planned maintenance windows shall be scheduled outside of these hours.
generated_requirements.rationale: OC-01 and Business Objective 3 mandate 99% availability during business hours to support live exercise operations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall complete each 60-second data refresh cycle without perceptible degradation in display performance or usability, maintaining full UI responsiveness throughout the refresh operation.
generated_requirements.rationale: OC-02 requires that the refresh cycle does not impair display performance or user experience.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall ensure that displayed data is never more than 60 seconds stale under normal operating conditions.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Objective 1 defines a maximum data staleness of 60 seconds as a measurable success criterion.

generated_requirements.text: The system's display layout, font sizes, and color-coded status indicators shall be legible and interpretable by at least 90% of viewers at a viewing distance of 100 feet on a 10×10 projection screen.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Objective 2 and TC-04 require projection-optimized readability at 100-foot viewing distances.

generated_requirements.text: The system's Event and Activity detail data shall be readable by at least 90% of viewers at a viewing distance of 30 feet on a 10×10 projection screen.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Objective 8 specifies a 30-foot readability standard for detailed Event/Activity data.

generated_requirements.text: At least 90% of new users shall be able to independently initiate an Event or Activity display within 90 minutes of first use, without formal training.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Objective 4 defines a measurable learnability criterion to minimize the learning curve for exercise controllers.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce role-based access control, ensuring that only users pre-authorized by the system administrator can access any part of the EEMDS application, with 100% enforcement of access decisions.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Objective 7 and BR-08 require zero unauthorized access incidents and 100% correct access control enforcement.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall comply with all applicable DHS IT security policies governing user authentication and access control for internal operational tools.
generated_requirements.rationale: RC-02 mandates compliance with DHS IT security policies for authentication and access control.

generated_requirements.text: The history report of Event and Activity data changes shall be complete and available for retrieval at the conclusion of each exercise, supporting full after-action reconstruction of the exercise timeline.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Objective 6 requires a complete change history report to be available at exercise conclusion for AAR analysis.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall consolidate and display all Events and Activities from both Sync Matrix 1.0 and EMT 1.0 in a single unified interface without requiring manual data entry by users.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Objective 5 requires automated consolidation of data from both source applications into a single display.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow the IT Administrator to manage user access authorization, including adding and removing authorized users, through an administrative interface or integration with an external directory service.
generated_requirements.rationale: The IT Administrator stakeholder role requires the ability to manage user authentication and access provisioning.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide display configuration capabilities, allowing authorized users to modify display settings such as time segment definitions and filter presets prior to or during exercise execution.
generated_requirements.rationale: The scope section includes display configuration and modification capabilities as an in-scope feature.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall visually distinguish the local time clock from the exercise time clock using separate labels, positions, or visual styling so that users can immediately identify each clock without ambiguity.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-09 requires the two clocks to be visually distinct to prevent confusion during exercise execution.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow filters (venue and key event) to be applied in combination, with the display updating to show only Events and Activities that satisfy all active filter criteria simultaneously.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-11 explicitly permits filters to be applied independently or in combination for flexible event isolation.

generated_requirements.text: The history report shall record, at minimum, the timestamp of each change, the identity of the source system in which the change originated, the Event or Activity identifier, and the nature of the data change.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-12 and the AAR Analyst stakeholder need require sufficient detail in the history log for meaningful post-exercise analysis.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall render the graphical timeline and data table simultaneously within a single MDI application window without requiring the user to switch between separate windows or views.
generated_requirements.rationale: TC-03 requires the MDI framework to support simultaneous display of both the timeline and data table in one window.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall indicate to the user when the data refresh cycle is paused, displaying a clear visual indicator that the currently shown data may not reflect the latest state from source systems.
generated_requirements.rationale: Users must be aware when the display is paused to avoid acting on stale data during exercise execution.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display the timestamp of the most recent successful data refresh so that users can assess data currency at any time.
generated_requirements.rationale: Displaying the last refresh timestamp supports situational awareness and helps users detect integration failures.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall notify the user with a visible alert if a data refresh cycle fails to retrieve data from one or both source applications, identifying which source is unavailable.
generated_requirements.rationale: D-01 and D-02 highlight dependency on source application availability; users must be informed of integration failures.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support the simultaneous display of Events and Activities sourced from both Sync Matrix 1.0 and EMT 1.0 within the same timeline and data table views, with each record attributed to its originating source.
generated_requirements.rationale: Consolidating data from both sources in a single view is a core objective and requires clear source attribution per BR-06.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall render correctly and maintain all readability standards when output to projection equipment displaying a 10×10 screen image connected to the client PC.
generated_requirements.rationale: TC-04 and OC-03 require the display to render correctly on projection equipment in an operations center environment.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall maintain a mean time to recovery (MTTR) of no more than 30 minutes for any unplanned outage occurring during normal business hours.
generated_requirements.rationale: Supporting the 99% availability objective requires rapid recovery from failures during live exercise operations.

generated_requirements.text: The system's codebase and configuration shall be structured to allow the IT Administrator to apply patches, updates, and configuration changes without requiring changes to source application interfaces or exercise data.
generated_requirements.rationale: Long-term operational sustainability requires that the system be maintainable by IT staff without disrupting integrations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall log all user authentication attempts, including successful logins, failed logins, and access denials, with timestamps and user identifiers, in a secure audit log.
generated_requirements.rationale: DHS IT security compliance and Business Objective 7 require a verifiable audit trail of all access attempts.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall encrypt all authentication credentials in transit and at rest, using encryption standards compliant with applicable DHS IT security policies.
generated_requirements.rationale: RC-02 mandates compliance with DHS IT security policies, which include protection of authentication credentials.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide the Exercise Planner role with the ability to pre-configure display settings, time segments, and filter presets before exercise execution begins.
generated_requirements.rationale: The Exercise Planner stakeholder requires pre-exercise configuration capabilities as described in the stakeholder needs and D-06.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow the AAR Analyst to export the history report of Event and Activity changes in a format suitable for post-exercise analysis and reporting (e.g., CSV, PDF, or structured data file).
generated_requirements.rationale: The AAR Analyst stakeholder requires access to change history in a usable format for after-action reporting.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall load and render the initial display, including timeline and data table populated with current exercise data, within 10 seconds of user login under normal operating conditions.
generated_requirements.rationale: Rapid initial load is essential for time-sensitive exercise operations where delays in display availability are unacceptable.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support concurrent use by multiple authorized users on separate client PCs within the exercise operations center without degradation in refresh performance or data consistency.
generated_requirements.rationale: Multiple stakeholders (controllers, directors, planners) may run the application simultaneously during exercise execution.

generated_requirements.text: The system's user interface shall provide contextual labels, tooltips, or inline guidance sufficient to enable a new user to understand the function of all primary controls without reference to external documentation.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Objective 4 requires 90% of new users to operate the system within 90 minutes without formal training, necessitating self-explanatory UI design.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow authenticated realtors to search MLS property listings by specifying criteria such as location, price range, property type, and other relevant attributes.
generated_requirements.rationale: Core field productivity feature enabling realtors to query MLS data without returning to the office.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display MLS property search results including clear property images and relevant listing details.
generated_requirements.rationale: Agents need visual and descriptive property information to advise clients on-site.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow authenticated realtors to create new property listings in the MLS from their mobile device.
generated_requirements.rationale: Enables field-based listing management, eliminating the need to return to the office to submit new listings.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow authenticated realtors to update existing MLS property listings from their mobile device.
generated_requirements.rationale: Supports real-time listing accuracy by allowing agents to make updates while in the field.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall validate all property listing data against defined MLS data validation rules before submitting any create or update operation to the MLS.
generated_requirements.rationale: Enforces BR-02 to prevent malformed or incorrect data from being introduced into MLS records.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall generate a Comparative Market Analysis (CMA) report based on property criteria explicitly specified by the realtor.
generated_requirements.rationale: Enables agents to provide on-the-spot market analysis to clients without office access.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall not generate a CMA report unless the realtor has explicitly provided the required property criteria.
generated_requirements.rationale: Enforces BR-08 to ensure CMA reports are always based on intentional, user-specified inputs.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display a map showing property locations for a realtor-specified geographic area.
generated_requirements.rationale: Provides spatial context for property listings, reducing reliance on separate mapping tools.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide driving directions to a selected property from within the application.
generated_requirements.rationale: Eliminates the need for agents to switch to separate navigation tools, streamlining field workflows.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall restrict map and driving direction functionality to properties that exist within the MLS dataset accessible to the authenticated realtor.
generated_requirements.rationale: Enforces BR-09 to ensure mapping features are scoped to authorized, MLS-verified property data.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall download, display, and allow management of client contacts synchronized from the office system.
generated_requirements.rationale: Eliminates reliance on paper notes or memory by giving agents access to current client data in the field.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall download, display, and allow management of appointments synchronized from the office system.
generated_requirements.rationale: Provides agents with up-to-date scheduling information without requiring office access.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall automatically synchronize contact and appointment data with the office system once every hour without requiring any action from the realtor.
generated_requirements.rationale: Enforces BR-03 to ensure field data is never more than 60 minutes stale.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall notify the realtor of any new appointments discovered during each automatic synchronization cycle.
generated_requirements.rationale: Enforces BR-06 to keep agents immediately informed of scheduling changes without manual checking.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall initiate calls to sellers or buyers to schedule appointments on behalf of the realtor, using contact phone numbers from the realtor's client records.
generated_requirements.rationale: Automates appointment coordination, reducing scheduling lag and manual follow-up effort.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall notify the realtor immediately when a seller or buyer responds to an appointment request initiated by the system, without requiring the realtor to manually check for updates.
generated_requirements.rationale: Enforces BR-06 to eliminate scheduling delays caused by missed or delayed client responses.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall restrict appointment-related communications to contacts associated exclusively with the authenticated realtor's client records.
generated_requirements.rationale: Enforces BR-10 to ensure communications are traceable and scoped to the correct realtor's client base.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall operate in an offline read-only mode when internet connectivity is unavailable, allowing realtors to view previously downloaded search results, CMA reports, and appointments.
generated_requirements.rationale: Enforces BR-04 to ensure agents remain productive in areas with poor or no connectivity.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall queue write operations that cannot be completed in offline mode and automatically submit them to the appropriate backend systems upon reconnection.
generated_requirements.rationale: Enforces BR-04 to prevent data loss and ensure eventual consistency when connectivity is restored.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow realtors to save property search results for later review, and saved results must remain accessible in offline mode.
generated_requirements.rationale: Enforces BR-07 to support agents who need to revisit search results without an active connection.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall save user preferences including search filters, display settings, and saved results, and restore them fully following any session end, system failure, or connectivity interruption.
generated_requirements.rationale: Enforces BR-05 to ensure agents do not lose personalized settings due to failures or disconnections.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall authenticate every user at the start of each session and authorize access only to realtors formally registered with the organization.
generated_requirements.rationale: Enforces BR-01 to ensure only authorized personnel can access proprietary MLS and client data.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall deny access to all system functions for any user who fails authentication or is not found in the registered realtor registry.
generated_requirements.rationale: Enforces BR-01 and the security constraint that zero unauthorized access incidents are tolerated.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a self-service installation process that an untrained realtor can complete on their smart device without printed instructions or IT assistance.
generated_requirements.rationale: Supports rapid onboarding and eliminates training overhead as required by the business objectives.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall run natively on Windows CE and Palm operating systems.
generated_requirements.rationale: The technical constraint specifies these as the only supported mobile platforms for this release.

generated_requirements.text: The system's user interface shall conform to the native UI standards and conventions of the target smart device platform, including standard menus and navigation buttons.
generated_requirements.rationale: Platform-native UI conventions reduce the learning curve and ensure intuitive operation for untrained realtors.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall return MLS property search results within 30 seconds of query submission, 100% of the time under normal operating conditions.
generated_requirements.rationale: A hard performance ceiling defined in the business objectives to ensure agents can respond to clients without delay.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall generate and deliver a CMA report within 60 seconds of the realtor completing criteria entry.
generated_requirements.rationale: A hard performance ceiling defined in the business objectives to enable on-the-spot market analysis.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall achieve a minimum availability of 99.9%, equating to no more than 1 failed access attempt per 1,000, operating 24 hours per day, 365 days per year.
generated_requirements.rationale: Realtors operate outside standard business hours and require continuous system access to serve clients.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support a minimum of 1,000 simultaneous authenticated users at launch without degradation of performance or availability.
generated_requirements.rationale: The current registered realtor population is 1,000 and all may be active concurrently in the field.

generated_requirements.text: The system architecture shall be scalable to support 5,000 simultaneous users within 12 months of launch without requiring a system redesign.
generated_requirements.rationale: The organization projects fivefold user growth within 12 months and the architecture must accommodate this without rework.

generated_requirements.text: At least 90% of untrained realtors shall be able to successfully install the application on their smart device without printed instructions.
generated_requirements.rationale: A measurable success criterion from the business objectives to validate self-service installation effectiveness.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be usable by realtors with no formal training on the product, using terminology and workflows natural to the real estate profession.
generated_requirements.rationale: Eliminates onboarding overhead and ensures immediate productivity for new agents joining the organization.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall restore 100% of saved user preferences to their last known state following any system failure or connectivity interruption.
generated_requirements.rationale: A measurable success criterion from the business objectives ensuring data integrity across failure events.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce authentication and authorization on every access attempt, ensuring zero unauthorized access incidents.
generated_requirements.rationale: Protects proprietary MLS and client data from unauthorized access as required by business rules and industry regulations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall store user preferences and cached data locally on the smart device to support offline operation.
generated_requirements.rationale: Local storage of cached data is the technical foundation for offline mode continuity when connectivity is lost.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce data validation controls on all MLS listing inputs to prevent incorrect or malformed data from being submitted to the MLS.
generated_requirements.rationale: Protects MLS data integrity as required by BR-02 and the security constraints of the BRD.

generated_requirements.text: All appointment-related communications initiated by the system (e.g., calls to buyers or sellers) shall comply with applicable telecommunications and consumer contact regulations.
generated_requirements.rationale: Regulatory compliance is mandated by Section 7.4 to avoid legal exposure from automated consumer communications.

generated_requirements.text: Access to MLS data shall be restricted to authenticated realtors who hold valid MLS membership, in compliance with real estate industry regulations.
generated_requirements.rationale: MLS access is governed by industry membership rules that must be enforced at the application level.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall integrate with the MLS programmatic interface (API) to support property listing queries, image retrieval, and submission of new or updated listings.
generated_requirements.rationale: MLS API integration is the foundational dependency for all property search and listing management features.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall integrate with the office system's synchronization interface (API or data feed) to perform hourly contact and appointment data synchronization.
generated_requirements.rationale: Office system integration is required to keep field data current and eliminate manual data retrieval.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall integrate with a licensed third-party mapping data provider to supply property location maps and driving directions.
generated_requirements.rationale: Third-party mapping integration is the dependency identified in A-06 for delivering navigation features within the app.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall utilize the smart device's native telephony capability to initiate calls to buyers or sellers for appointment scheduling.
generated_requirements.rationale: Native telephony integration is required per A-07 to support the automated appointment communication feature.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall log all appointment-related communications initiated on behalf of a realtor, traceable to that realtor's account.
generated_requirements.rationale: Enforces BR-10 traceability requirement and supports audit and compliance needs for consumer communications.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall transition automatically between online and offline modes based on the availability of internet connectivity, without requiring manual intervention from the realtor.
generated_requirements.rationale: Seamless mode switching ensures uninterrupted agent productivity regardless of connectivity changes in the field.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall clearly indicate to the realtor when it is operating in offline mode and which features are unavailable due to lack of connectivity.
generated_requirements.rationale: Transparent status communication prevents agent confusion and sets correct expectations about available functionality.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall recover gracefully from failures, restoring all locally cached data, queued operations, and user preferences to their last known state upon restart.
generated_requirements.rationale: Graceful recovery is essential to meet the 100% preference restoration success criterion and maintain agent trust.

generated_requirements.text: The system's backend infrastructure shall be provisioned and maintained to support between 1,000 and 5,000 concurrent users, including authentication, synchronization, and middleware services.
generated_requirements.rationale: Backend capacity must match the projected user growth to sustain performance and availability SLAs.

generated_requirements.text: The system's codebase and architecture shall be structured to allow independent scaling of backend components (authentication, synchronization, MLS integration) without full system redeployment.
generated_requirements.rationale: Modular, independently scalable components reduce the cost and risk of accommodating the projected fivefold user growth.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall use secure, encrypted communication channels for all data transmitted between the mobile application and backend systems, including authentication credentials, MLS data, and contact information.
generated_requirements.rationale: Encryption of data in transit protects sensitive MLS and client data from interception over cellular or Wi-Fi networks.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow Program Administrators to define and modify a preferred class sequence — including the quarter in which each class will be offered — for each cohort.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce that only students who are eligible for a cohort's Program of Study may be added to that cohort.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow Program Administrators and Nursing Staff Members to add and modify nursing student records, including personal information, enrollment status (full-time or part-time), program enrollment, and cohort assignment.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall classify every class as either a non-clinical class or a clinical class, and shall not permit a class to be saved without one of these two classifications.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall require that a non-clinical class record specifies, at minimum, the course name, lecture room requirements, and instructor needs before the record can be saved.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall require that a clinical class record specifies, at minimum, the course name, lecture room requirements, clinical site needs, lecture instructor needs, and clinical lab instructor needs before the record can be saved.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall automatically detect scheduling conflicts between classes assigned to the same cohort and shall prevent any conflicting schedule from being saved.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow Program Administrators to create, modify, and cancel clinical lab sections, where each section is associated with an existing clinical class and specifies the clinical site name, assigned instructor, and day and time of the lab.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall prevent the cancellation of a clinical lab section if any students are currently enrolled in that section, and shall display an informative error message explaining the reason.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow staff members to enroll a student in a clinical lab section only if the student is already registered for the corresponding clinical class.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow staff members to move a student from one clinical lab section to another, provided both sections correspond to the same clinical class.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall maintain a centralized repository of clinical site records, including site name, contact person name, and contact information, accessible only to authorized users.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow authorized users to view all clinical lab sections scheduled at a given clinical site for any specified quarter.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall automatically send email notifications to all affected parties when any of the following events occur: a student's schedule is published or updated; a clinical section's capacity changes or is cancelled; a class is cancelled or its details change; a cohort's sequence or weekly schedule is modified; a student's status changes; or a student is moved to a different clinical section.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall use stored email addresses as the sole primary channel for all outbound automated communications.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall maintain a contact information repository storing email addresses and phone numbers for all relevant parties, including staff, students, lecture instructors, clinical lab instructors, and clinical site administrators.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall generate on-demand cross-cohort quarterly planning reports that list all classes needed for a specified quarter, including sections, labs, and room types, without requiring manual data aggregation.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall generate printable summary reports for individual cohorts, clinical sites, clinical lab sections, and individual students.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a single, unified application through which all dispute cases — ticket retrieval requests and chargeback notifications — are created, managed, and resolved.
generated_requirements.rationale: Centralizing dispute processing eliminates the fragmented, multi-system workflow identified as a primary operational pain point.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall authenticate all users prior to granting access, permitting logon only to users with valid, active accounts.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-02 mandates that only authenticated users with active accounts may access the system to prevent unauthorized access.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce role-based access control with at minimum two access levels: View (search and view cases only) and Update (create and modify cases).
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-01 requires that only Update-level users can initiate or modify disputes, while View-only users are restricted to read operations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall restrict each user's access to dispute cases belonging exclusively to their authorized cardholder base, blocking any cross-portfolio access.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-03 prohibits cross-portfolio access to protect cardholder data and prevent unauthorized case viewing or modification.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall prohibit external users from requesting original receipts; this action shall be available to internal users only.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-04 explicitly restricts original receipt requests to internal users, preventing external partner misuse.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow only the System Administrator to reinstate a revoked user account; no other user role shall have this capability.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-05 ensures that account reinstatement is a controlled, auditable action limited to the System Administrator.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enable the System Administrator to add new users, assign and modify access levels, revoke accounts, and reinstate revoked accounts.
generated_requirements.rationale: The System Administrator requires full user lifecycle management capabilities to maintain access control integrity.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall dynamically filter the list of available follow-up actions presented to a user based on (a) the current status of the dispute case and (b) the access level of the logged-in user.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-06 requires context-sensitive action filtering to prevent users from performing unauthorized or inapplicable actions on a case.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall present only transactions that fall within the eligible dispute age window, as defined by applicable regulations, when a user initiates a dispute.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-07 mandates that only disputable transactions are surfaced to users, ensuring regulatory compliance on dispute eligibility.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support the initiation of a single dispute case covering multiple transactions, provided all selected transactions belong to a single merchant.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-08 allows multi-transaction disputes against one merchant to reflect real-world dispute scenarios and reduce case proliferation.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall automatically detect and block the creation of a duplicate dispute request on any transaction for which a dispute case already exists.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-09 requires 100% duplicate prevention to avoid erroneous financial adjustments and reconciliation problems.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support three types of ticket retrieval requests: (1) Request for Original Receipt, (2) Request for a Copy of the Receipt, and (3) Request for a Portfolio, each with its own eligibility rules.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-10 defines the three distinct retrieval request types that must be supported with type-specific eligibility enforcement.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support two types of chargeback requests — Pending and Immediate — and shall prompt the user for all required information specific to the selected chargeback type.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-11 requires both chargeback types with type-specific data collection to ensure complete and accurate case creation.

generated_requirements.text: Upon successful creation of a ticket retrieval request, the system shall display a confirmation containing the dispute case number, the type of retrieval requested, and the date the merchant response is due.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-12 mandates immediate confirmation with key case details to give representatives actionable information upon case creation.

generated_requirements.text: Upon successful creation of a chargeback request, the system shall display a confirmation containing the dispute case number, the chargeback type (Pending or Immediate), and the date the merchant response is due.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-13 mandates immediate confirmation with key case details to give representatives actionable information upon chargeback creation.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall dynamically derive the list of available dispute reason codes based on (a) the type of transaction being disputed and (b) the industry classification of the merchant involved; generic or unconstrained reason code selection shall not be permitted.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-14 requires context-sensitive reason code filtering to ensure regulatory compliance and data accuracy in dispute classification.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall automatically record every user-initiated and system-initiated action on a dispute case in the case history, capturing the user's name or system identifier, the date of the action, and a description of the action taken.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-15 mandates a complete, tamper-evident audit trail for every dispute case to support internal auditing and regulatory examination.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall record all additions of new users and all modifications to existing users' access levels in a User Report that is available for audit review.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-16 requires a User Report to provide auditors with a complete history of access control changes.

generated_requirements.text: When an automated system process acts on a dispute case, the system shall notify the user who originally initiated that dispute case upon their next logon.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-17 ensures representatives are made aware of automated case changes in a timely manner to prevent missed follow-up actions.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall automatically purge dispute cases that have been closed for more than 6 months from the online disputes database via a systematic, automated process.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-18 mandates automated data retention management to maintain system performance and control storage costs.

generated_requirements.text: All business rules implemented in the Disputes System shall comply with the guidelines of Regulation E and Regulation Z governing credit card dispute processing.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-19 and C-01 impose a legal compliance constraint requiring all system behavior to conform to federal credit card dispute regulations.

generated_requirements.text: All business rules implemented in the Disputes System shall comply with the Merchant Operating Regulations governing chargeback procedures and retrieval request handling.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-20 and C-02 impose a compliance constraint requiring adherence to merchant-side dispute processing regulations.

generated_requirements.text: All letter requests generated by the Disputes System shall be formatted in accordance with the specifications required by the Print Letter Utility application and submitted exclusively through that utility.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-21 and C-07 require that letter generation is delegated to the Print Letter Utility to maintain integration integrity and prevent independent letter printing.

generated_requirements.text: All financial adjustment requests to cardmember or merchant accounts shall be submitted exclusively through the Cardmember Posting and Billing System; the Disputes application shall not post adjustments directly to any account.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-22 and C-08 mandate that financial adjustments flow through the authoritative billing system to ensure accuracy and auditability.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall integrate in real time with the Statement Database to retrieve transaction and statement data for dispute initiation and case management workflows.
generated_requirements.rationale: C-05 requires all transaction data to be sourced from the Statement Database, and real-time integration eliminates manual re-keying errors.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall integrate in real time with the Cardmember Information Database to retrieve cardmember account data; the Disputes application shall not maintain its own copy of cardmember master data.
generated_requirements.rationale: C-04 mandates exclusive sourcing of cardmember data from the authoritative Cardmember Information Database.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall integrate in real time with the Merchant Information Database to retrieve merchant data; the Disputes application shall not maintain its own copy of merchant master data.
generated_requirements.rationale: C-06 mandates exclusive sourcing of merchant data from the authoritative Merchant Information Database.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide dispute case search functionality allowing users to search by dispute case number, merchant account number, cardmember account number, and issuer number.
generated_requirements.rationale: Multiple search dimensions are required to support the varied lookup needs of cardmember and merchant services representatives.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support filtering of dispute case search results by date range, dispute type, case status, and reason code.
generated_requirements.rationale: Advanced filtering enables representatives and supervisors to quickly isolate relevant cases within large portfolios.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display dispute case search results with color-coded status indicators, including at minimum yellow for open cases and gray for closed cases, to enable rapid visual identification of case status.
generated_requirements.rationale: Color-coded results provide real-time visual case status visibility, replacing the current manual effort required to identify open or aging cases.

generated_requirements.text: End users shall not have direct access to any underlying data files or databases; all data interaction shall occur exclusively through the Disputes application's user interface.
generated_requirements.rationale: C-09 mandates UI-only data access to prevent unauthorized direct database manipulation and maintain data integrity.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support a minimum of 350 concurrent users without measurable performance degradation.
generated_requirements.rationale: C-10 and the success criteria require the system to handle the organization's full concurrent user load without degradation.

generated_requirements.text: Screen-to-screen navigation response time shall not exceed 5 seconds under any load condition within the supported concurrency range of 350 concurrent users.
generated_requirements.rationale: C-11 sets a hard 5-second response time ceiling to ensure representatives can work efficiently without system-induced delays.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be available 24 hours a day, 7 days a week, 365 days a year, with the only permitted downtime being scheduled maintenance windows: Monday through Saturday, 3:00 AM to 4:00 AM EST, and Sunday, 1:00 AM to 5:00 AM EST.
generated_requirements.rationale: C-12 mandates near-continuous availability to support the organization's 24-hour multi-user operational environment.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be accessible by both internal users (employees) and external users (partner institution representatives), with role-appropriate access restrictions enforced for each group.
generated_requirements.rationale: C-13 requires the system to serve both user populations while maintaining strict access differentiation between them.

generated_requirements.text: The system's user interface shall comply with the organization's corporate standards for user interface creation, applicable to both internally and externally used applications.
generated_requirements.rationale: C-03 mandates adherence to corporate UI standards to ensure consistency, accessibility, and organizational compliance.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be sufficiently intuitive that 100% of cardmember and merchant services representatives can adopt it as their primary dispute tool following a 2-day training course.
generated_requirements.rationale: C-14 places a binding usability constraint on the system design to ensure full adoption within the defined training investment.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be designed such that 100% of cardmember services representatives can successfully create a dispute case on their first attempt after completing the 2-day training course.
generated_requirements.rationale: The success criteria require first-attempt success for dispute creation, placing a high learnability standard on the initiation workflow.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be designed such that 100% of merchant services representatives can successfully perform a follow-up action on a dispute case on their first attempt after completing the 2-day training course.
generated_requirements.rationale: The success criteria require first-attempt success for follow-up actions, placing a high learnability standard on the case management workflow.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall block 100% of unauthorized access attempts, ensuring no user can access functionality or data outside their assigned role and cardholder base.
generated_requirements.rationale: The security success criteria require zero unauthorized access to pass through the system's access control mechanisms.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall record a complete, tamper-evident audit trail such that 100% of user-initiated and system-initiated actions on every dispute case are captured with the acting user's name or system identifier, date, and action detail.
generated_requirements.rationale: A complete audit trail is required to support regulatory examination and eliminate the legal exposure created by the current lack of automated case history.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce that letter requests are automatically formatted and submitted to the Print Letter Utility as an integrated step within dispute initiation and follow-up workflows, without requiring manual intervention by the representative.
generated_requirements.rationale: Automating letter submission within the workflow eliminates the coordination gaps and delays caused by the current out-of-band letter request process.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall automatically submit financial adjustment requests to the Cardmember Posting and Billing System as an integrated step within dispute workflows, without requiring manual intervention by the representative.
generated_requirements.rationale: Automating adjustment submission within the workflow eliminates coordination gaps and ensures adjustments are processed through the authoritative billing system.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide the System Administrator with the ability to generate a User Report listing all user account additions and access level modifications, available for audit review at any time.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-16 and the audit success criteria require a readily accessible User Report to support internal and regulatory audits of access control changes.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall maintain reliable operation and data integrity such that zero duplicate dispute requests are created on the same transaction across all concurrent user sessions.
generated_requirements.rationale: The success criteria require 100% duplicate prevention, which must hold even under concurrent access conditions to prevent financial reconciliation errors.

generated_requirements.text: The automated data purge process for dispute cases closed more than 6 months shall execute reliably on a scheduled basis without manual intervention and without impacting system availability or performance.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-18 requires a systematic, automated purge; reliability of this process is essential to maintaining database hygiene and system performance over time.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be maintainable such that updates to business rules — including reason code eligibility, dispute age windows, and regulatory compliance rules — can be implemented without requiring changes to core application architecture.
generated_requirements.rationale: Regulatory rules and business logic are subject to change; a maintainable rule configuration reduces the cost and risk of future compliance updates.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be designed to support integration with the Statement Database, Cardmember Information Database, Merchant Information Database, Print Letter Utility, and Cardmember Posting and Billing System through well-defined, documented interfaces that do not require modification of those external systems.
generated_requirements.rationale: Clean integration boundaries ensure the Disputes application can be maintained and upgraded independently of the external systems it interfaces with.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide guided, step-by-step workflows for dispute case initiation (both ticket retrieval and chargeback types) with real-time prompts for required information at each step.
generated_requirements.rationale: Guided workflows reduce representative error rates, support first-attempt success, and enforce complete data collection required for regulatory compliance.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display a notification to the originating representative upon their next logon for each dispute case on which an automated system process has acted since their last logon, identifying the affected case and the nature of the automated action.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-17 requires timely notification of automated case actions to ensure representatives can take necessary follow-up steps and prevent missed chargeback deadlines.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a web-based recycled parts search function accessible exclusively to users with the Collision Estimator role.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-01 and BO-1 require that only collision estimators can search for recycled parts to standardize and control the sourcing workflow.

generated_requirements.text: The recycled parts search shall accept vehicle information and damaged parts information as search inputs and query the ChoiceParts data feed for matching available parts.
generated_requirements.rationale: The future-state capability requires estimators to search by vehicle and parts data against the ChoiceParts integration.

generated_requirements.text: The recycled parts search shall first query the estimator's list of preferred parts suppliers; if no results are found, the system shall automatically expand the search to all suppliers within a 30-mile radius of the vehicle's ZIP code.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-05 and BR-06 define the two-stage preferred-supplier-then-radius fallback search logic.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall prevent an estimator from applying recycled parts to a collision estimate when the recycled parts search returns no available parts.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-07 mandates that parts cannot be applied if none are available, ensuring data integrity of the estimate.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall persist the recycled parts search results associated with each estimate so that they can be retrieved during the audit process at a later time.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-08 requires retained search results to enable accurate audit comparison between available and applied parts.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a preferred repair facility search function that accepts vehicle year, make, model, vehicle location (street address, city, state, ZIP code), and a search radius between 1 and 30 miles as inputs.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-09 specifies the required inputs for the preferred repair facility search to ensure location- and vehicle-relevant results.

generated_requirements.text: When the vehicle ZIP code is not provided, the system shall automatically resolve the ZIP code from the street address, city, and state before executing the repair facility search.

generated_requirements.text: The recycled parts search shall return results to the user within 15 seconds of query submission under normal operating conditions.
